label: The Refraction Cask
description: The Refraction Cask occupies a wedge-cut chamber set between a corridor of glare and a maintained trench of absolute shadow, its prismatic walls ground to strict meridian angles. A stepped glass bar runs the long face of the room, backed by bottle-niches carved into dark crystalline strata that absorb stray light and sharpen color into hard bands. The ceiling is ribbed with lattice-aligned louvers that split radiance into fixed spectrums, laying a permanent map of hues across tables of fused shardglass. Acoustic baffles of faceted crystal dampen echoes, giving the interior a disciplined quiet despite its reflective surfaces.
